# UC-19.2.36 · oVirt / RHV Storage Domain Used Percent and Low-Space Warning

## Description

oVirt blocks new disk operations when domains enter low-space conditions. Trending `used_percent` and absolute free bytes highlights domains approaching maintenance thresholds before snapshots or template imports fail.

## Value

Avoids surprise storage-domain lockouts during VM provisioning and backup merges.

## Implementation

Poll `/api/storagedomains` and compute utilization from `used` and `available` if `used_percent` is not provided. Alert at tiered thresholds (warn 80%, critical 90%). Exclude ISO domains if they are intentionally full. Correlate with SAN thin reclaim jobs.

## SPL

```spl
index=virtualization sourcetype="ovirt:storagedomain" earliest=-4h
| where used_percent>=85 OR available<536870912000
| stats latest(used_percent) as pct, latest(available) as avail_bytes, latest(status) as st by name
| eval avail_tb=round(avail_bytes/1099511627776,2)
| table name, pct, avail_tb, st
| sort -pct
```

## Visualization

Gauge (used %), Table (domains sorted by utilization), Timechart (pct trend).

## References

- [oVirt Admin Guide — Storage domains](https://www.ovirt.org/documentation/admin-guide/chap-Storage.html)
